meta_title: For Freelancers: debugging with AI to Solve no version control (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Debugging with AI to Fix No Version Control
content.seo_description: Stop losing code. Learn to use AI-driven debugging with Antigravity and Claude Code to recover and manage unversioned projects like a pro.
content.answer_block: When version control is absent, AI becomes your safety net. By leveraging Claude Code to analyze snapshots and Antigravity to map dependency logic, you can reverse-engineer your progress, identify broken states, and establish a robust recovery workflow that turns chaotic, unversioned directories into production-ready, stable codebases instantly.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, the client is waiting, and you just realized your project folder is a graveyard of 'final_v2_new_final' files without a single Git commit to save you. We have all been there—staring at a broken deployment, paralyzed by the fear that one wrong keystroke will erase the last six hours of work. It is the freelancer's nightmare: zero version control, high stakes, and a deadline that refuses to move.

But what if you didn't need a history log to reconstruct the truth? By treating your current code state as a forensic puzzle, you can use AI to bridge the gap. This isn't just about fixing bugs; it's about building a recovery pipeline that makes version control mistakes a thing of the past.

steps.title: Establish an AI-Readable Context
steps.description: Before debugging, you must convert your messy workspace into a structured format that AI can parse effectively.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current directory structure and ignore node_modules. Create a dependency map of the main entry point to identify where the logic flow breaks.

steps.title: Automated Forensic Analysis
steps.description: Use Claude Code to identify the 'drift' between your expected output and the current broken state.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Compare current file state against standard best practices for this framework. Highlight logic gaps that would typically be caught by version control diffs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'identify logic drift in current directory'

steps.title: Isolate the Breaking Change
steps.description: Use AI to surgically extract and test the specific block of code that introduced the instability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Find the most recent functional change in this file. Propose a revert strategy that maintains current feature progress.

steps.title: Implement Defensive Debugging
steps.description: Inject AI-generated logging and assertions to prevent further regressions in the absence of a repo history.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate defensive error-handling wrappers for the identified volatile functions to ensure runtime stability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply 'add defensive error logging to identified volatile functions'

steps.title: Establish a 'Pseudo-Version' Baseline
steps.description: Create a manual snapshot of your project state to serve as a temporary source of truth.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a summary manifest of all current files and their functional dependencies to act as a project checkpoint.

pro_tips: Always run your AI debug sessions in a sub-folder copy to prevent accidental overwrites when you lack Git history.
Use Claude Code's 'memory' feature to store business logic constraints, so the AI remembers your client's specific requirements across different files.
Treat your AI chat history as your commit log; export the session as a markdown file to keep a 'pseudo-changelog' for the client.
If the code is too messy, ask the AI to refactor it into a single-responsibility module before attempting to fix the bug.
Use Antigravity to generate documentation for your functions; it acts as a self-documenting safety net when you can't rely on Git blame.
content.next_steps: Once you have stabilized your code, set up a basic GitHub repository immediately. Use the 'manifest' you generated in Step 5 to create your initial commit.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting unversioned code dictate your career. Master these AI workflows to ship faster, debug with confidence, and impress your clients every single time. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
